method: market scan -> mispricing score -> stock-vs-LEAPS choice -> ranked picks

rows.name: MP equity on magnet sovereignty and humanoid demand
rows.thesis: MP Materials sits on the domestic rare-earth magnet sovereignty theme, with AI infrastructure, robotics, EVs, and defense all tightening attention on high-performance magnet supply.
rows.bottleneck_layer: Heavy rare earth and permanent magnet manufacturing
why_missed: market still treats rare earths as a commodity story instead of a sovereignty chokepoint
robotics and defense demand are not yet fully embedded in consensus narratives
catalysts: further export-control pressure on the ex-China chain
invalidations: alternative supply scales faster than expected

mispricing.candidate_name: MP equity on magnet sovereignty and humanoid demand
mispricing.explanation: MP equity on magnet sovereignty and humanoid demand scores 83.6/100 (critical) on mispricing plausibility. The strongest weighted drivers are recognition gap, hiddenness, catalyst clarity. The least supportive signals are crowding inverse, valuation nonlinearity. This screen is a structural ranking aid, not live options pricing.

options_fit.candidate_name: MP equity on magnet sovereignty and humanoid demand
options_fit.explanation: MP equity on magnet sovereignty and humanoid demand scores 70.6/100 (high) on options-expression fit. rows.name: HUBB equity on transformer lead-time persistence
rows.thesis: Hubbell sits near the grid hard-goods bottleneck created by AI load growth, utility capex, and long transformer and related equipment lead times.
rows.bottleneck_layer: Utility-scale power transformers and related grid equipment
rows.value_capture_layer: Grid hardware supplier with pricing power and replacement demand
why_missed: grid capex stories remain less exciting than semis and GPUs
AI demand is often modeled at the compute layer, not the power-delivery layer
